exemplar.title: How does Sylvia Plath portray 1950s gender norms through Esther Greenwoodâs struggle in her novel The Bell Jar?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does Sylvia Plath portray 1950s gender norms through Esther Greenwoodâs struggle in her novel The Bell Jar?

subject.title: English B
subject.description:
**IB English B** is a **Group 2** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **language acquisition through five prescribed themes: identities, experiences, human ingenuity, social organization, and sharing the planet**, with a strong focus on **developing receptive, productive, and interactive communication skills in English as a second language**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Paper 1** | Productive skills - writing | 1 hour | 1 hour 15 minutes | 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Receptive skills - listening and reading comprehension | 1 hour 45 minutes | 2 hours | 50% |
| **Individual Oral** | Internal assessment - oral communication | 15 min prep + 10 min exam | 15 min prep + 10 min exam | 25% |

strengths: The investigationâs focus and purpose are communicated with precision and consistency throughout, with a clearly stated, wellâintegrated research question anchoring each section.
The framing and organization of the essay demonstrate a highly effective and appropriate methodological approach, with logical paragraphing and a coherent progression of ideas.
weaknesses: Minor background digressions occasionally slow the narrative flow and could be more tightly signposted to the research question.
The methodology section would benefit from an explicit statement of sourceâselection criteria to underpin the strong organization already present.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the strand assessment placed this criterion at the lower end of the top band, the clarity of purpose, sustained connection to the research question, and overall structural coherence merit the full 6 marks. The minor lapses are outweighed by the consistent communicative strength of the introduction and body.

strengths: Source material is clearly relevant and is integrated with accuracy, drawing on both primary and secondary texts to support key arguments.
Subject knowledge is coherent and well structured, demonstrating sound command of Plathâs symbolism and the historical context of 1950s gender norms.
Terminology and concepts are used appropriately and consistently, enhancing the precision of the argument.
weaknesses: Several background sources are deployed descriptively rather than critically and would benefit from deeper appraisal of their authority or limitations.
A few claims (e.g., on birth rates or character motivations) require more precise evidence or citation to avoid generalisation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay demonstrates clear and coherent knowledge, with mostly effective application of sources. A score of 5 (one point above the middle band) acknowledges the strong moments of analysis and terminology use, while reflecting the need for more critical engagement with some sources.

strengths: Research is broad, varied and consistently linked to the research question, drawing on an extensive bibliography of literary criticism, historical archives, and socioâcultural studies.
Analysis generally shows depth, blending historical context and textual close reading to support reasoned conclusions about Estherâs struggle and 1950s gender norms.
The discussion builds a coherent argument, synthesizing findings in a focused way and demonstrating persuasive evaluation of thematic and symbolic elements.
weaknesses: Some analytical passages lapse into narrative summary, and alternative interpretations or counterarguments are rarely explored in depth.
Occasional logical leaps (for instance, the link to Disney films or the Rosenberg parallel) require more sustained corroboration from secondary scholarship.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the strand assessments placed the work in the 4â6 band, its sustained relevance, breadth of research, and overall coherence of argument justify elevating it into the midârange of the 7â9 band. A score of 9 reflects strong critical engagement, balanced by the few analytical inconsistencies noted.

strengths: The report follows the prescribed structure flawlessly, with all required elements (title page, contents, sections, conclusion, works cited) clearly and logically sequenced.
Layout conventions (footnotes, consistent heading styles) are correctly applied, ensuring ease of navigation and professional presentation.
weaknesses: The dotâleader formatting in the table of contents and occasional spacing around block quotations are slightly inconsistent.
The final reference on its own page disrupts visual flow and could be integrated more smoothly.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation is strong overall, meeting the top band descriptor fully. Minor layout inconsistencies do not impede readability, warranting the maximum 4 marks for this criterion.

strandSelections.reasoning: **The research question is explicit, focused and sustained.** It is phrased as: âHow does Sylvia Plath portray 1950s gender norms through Esther Greenwoodâs struggle in *The Bell Jar*?â  â¢  The wording is sufficiently narrow to be addressed in 4,000 words and directly anchors every main section of the discussion.  â¢  Each body chapter maps back to elements of that question (historical context, specific characters, symbolism), demonstrating a firm connection between the RQ and ensuing analysis.  A few tangential historical digressions mean the focus is not always razor-sharp, so the score sits at 5 rather than 6.

**Analysis is coherent and usually linked to evidence, but depth fluctuates.** Close readings of Estherâs relationships, the fig-tree metaphor, and Rosenberg parallels demonstrate thoughtful interpretation that supports the thesis.  â¢  The student blends historical context with textual analysis to build reasoned points.  â¢  Yet several passages lapse into narrative summary or descriptive background, and counter-arguments are seldom explored.  Minor logical leaps (e.g., Disney linkage) slightly weaken the chain of reasoning, placing the work at 5 within the 4-6 band.

**Source material is generally pertinent and integrated, though occasionally descriptive.** The essay quotes *The Bell Jar* effectively and supports claims with historical scholarship (e.g., GI Bill, post-war suburbia) that illuminates gender expectations.  â¢  Citations are accurate and varied, showing engagement with both primary and secondary texts.  â¢  Nonetheless, several sources are deployed to provide background rather than to develop a critical argument, and some references (e.g., Disney films) are asserted rather than analysed.  This places performance solidly in, but not beyond, the 3-4 band.

**Most layout requirements are met, but some elements are imperfect.** The essay states the word count, includes a clear title, and presents a contents page.  â¢  In-text footnotes observe a consistent style, but page numbering is not visible in the supplied extract and some indentation/spacing around quotations is irregular.  â¢  Visual formatting therefore shows âsome missing or incorrect elements,â matching the 1-2 band, and merits the upper score of 2.

**Clear awareness of literary and historical content is demonstrated.** The student explains Plathâs symbolism (the bell jar, fig tree) and situates the novel within McCarthy-era America and post-war domestic ideology, indicating a competent command of subject knowledge.  â¢  References to feminist theory and social history show breadth, while detailed textual commentary shows depth.  â¢  However, some claims remain surface-level or unsubstantiated (e.g., Jay Ceeâs career cost), and the essay does not fully engage with existing critical debates.  Consequently, the strand aligns with the middle band.

**The report follows the prescribed EE structure effectively.** Title page, abstract substitute, table of contents, labelled sections, conclusion and full works-cited list are all present.  â¢  Sequencing of material is logical and supports reader navigation.  â¢  Minor issues (slightly uneven section lengths, sporadic over-long paragraphs) prevent a flawless presentation but still meet the descriptor for the top band (3-4), warranting the maximum 4 points within that band.

**Research is broad and generally well aligned with the RQ.** The works-cited list includes 20+ items spanning literary criticism, sociological studies and historical archives, showing deliberate effort to build a substantive base.  â¢  Most sources directly illuminate aspects of 1950s gender ideology or Plathâs text, demonstrating clear though not unwavering relevance.  â¢  Some secondary items (e.g., museum blog posts) are of limited academic weight, and the essay rarely challenges or contrasts sources, so it does not reach the âexcellentâ 7-9 band but does merit the top of the adequate 4-6 band.

**Clarity and purpose are consistently evident.** The student frames the investigation around a concise, literature-focused exploration of 1950s gender norms in Plathâs novel and reiterates that focus throughout the essay.  â¢  The introduction clearly states the cultural context, justifies the relevance of *The Bell Jar*, and signals how historical material will be woven into textual analysis.  â¢  Paragraphing and a logical progression of ideas enable the reader to follow the central line of inquiry without confusion.  Minor digressions (e.g., extended background on household appliances) do not significantly blur the focal aim, but they prevent the communication from being exemplary, keeping performance at the lower end of the top band.

**Discussion is generally clear with some evaluation, but conclusions are uneven.** The student draws connections between societal expectations and Estherâs breakdown, and the conclusion reflects on generational impact, indicating an ability to synthesise findings.  â¢  However, evaluation of source reliability or alternative interpretations is limited, and occasional repetition reduces sharpness.  â¢  Argumentation remains well-structured enough to sit comfortably in the upper half of the 4-6 band without reaching the sophistication of bands 7-9 or 10-12.

**A mostly sound but not fully systematic methodology is evident.** The student identifies a primary text and one additional primary source (Asheâs *Secret Agents* chapter) plus a range of secondary historical articles.  â¢  Source selection is generally relevant, and MLA-style footnotes and a works-cited list are supplied.  â¢  However, there is no explicit explanation of selection criteria, nor is there evidence of contrasting scholarly interpretations or a planned analytical framework.  As a result, the methodology is âmostly completeâ rather than comprehensive, meriting the upper end of the 3-4 band.

**Literary and historical terminology is mostly accurate and appropriate.** Terms such as âpatriarchal oppression,â âsecond-wave feminism,â âconfessional narrative,â and âsymbolismâ are used correctly and add precision to the argument.  â¢  Occasional stylistic slips (e.g., âsouthernerâ for âSoutherner,â or minor syntactic awkwardness) prevent complete consistency, but overall usage is sound, aligning with the descriptor for band 3-4.

annotations.comment: Several URLs are presented in full. Consider using shortened links or omitting long URLs to maintain a clean reference list.

The title effectively signals scope, text and lensââAn Analysis of Gender Normsâ¦Through the Lens of Esther Greenwoodâs Struggleâ clearly communicates purpose and focus, anchoring the investigation toward a targeted literary and historical study.
The research question is explicit and well-focused: it names the author, novel, era and thematic concern, providing a clear anchor for all subsequent analysis.
The table of contents is present but the dot-leader formatting is inconsistent. Applying uniform leaders and right-aligned page numbers will improve navigation and visual cohesion.
The opening paragraph supplies rich context but reads descriptively. Tightening background and clearly signposting the link to your research question will strengthen focus.
Web-based historical sources are integrated descriptively but lack critical appraisal. Discussing their authority or limitations will enhance the depth of source application.
Plot summary in this section dominates over analysis. Focusing more on how these plot details directly illustrate 1950s gender norms will deepen your argument.

Methodology is mentioned informally but lacks an explicit criteria or analytical framework. Including a dedicated methods subsection with selection rationale and planned analytical steps will improve clarity and rigor.
Historical context is thorough yet detached from Plathâs text. Bridging each background detail with direct evidence from The Bell Jar will reinforce relevance to your central argument.
The claim about birth rates among educated women would benefit from a specific statistic or citation to support the assertion and avoid generalisation.
Nagowskiâs thesis is cited but its limitations or counterarguments are not considered. Evaluating the sourceâs methodology and potential bias will strengthen research quality.
The inclusion of an accurate word count at 3,990 words demonstrates adherence to the 4,000-word limit and satisfies IB formatting requirements.
The bibliography mixes MLA and Chicago styles. Adopting a consistent citation style throughout is essential for a polished academic presentation.
The final reference stands alone on its own page, disrupting continuity. Combining it with the previous page will improve layout flow.
The discussion of the âMrs Degreeâ stereotype is compelling but could be deepened by linking to established feminist scholarship on womenâs educational choices.
The claim that Estherâs mother drives her suicidal impulse is significant but would be more convincing if supported by additional textual quotations or psychological analysis.
Your analysis of Buddy Williard is insightful. To deepen critical thinking, contrast this portrayal with secondary scholarship on male archetypes in 1950s literature.
This paragraph largely recaps plot events. Focusing instead on how specific moments reflect societal gender norms will yield a more rigorous argument.
The interpretation of sexual liberation and blood symbolism is provocative but needs support from scholarly criticism to validate the reading.
The contrast between Esther and Doreen is well articulated, illustrating the spectrum of female responses to patriarchal expectations in the text.
Your reading of Jay Cee offers a nuanced view of workplace femininity. Integrating external studies on mid-century career women could further substantiate this analysis.
The connection between Esther Greenwoodâs name and Ethel Rosenberg is compelling. Presenting alternative interpretations will demonstrate critical engagement and strengthen your argument.
The birth imagery analysis is vivid; to deepen discussion, consider referencing relevant psychoanalytic or feminist readings on childbirth representations.
Linking suburban growth and female isolation adds valuable societal context to Estherâs psychological landscape, demonstrating clear relevance to the research question.
The Mumford quote is used twice in quick succession. Ensure correct and varied citation formatting or introduce additional sources to avoid repetition.
The fig tree passage is central to your thesis. Structuring the section as an extended close reading will sharpen analytical depth and coherence.
While you unpack the symbolism effectively, comparing it to other metaphors in critical literature will demonstrate broader engagement with scholarly debate.
The works-cited entries vary in italicisation and punctuation. Standardising this to a single MLA format will improve overall presentation.
Some entries lack publisher information and page ranges. Completing these details will ensure each reference meets IB bibliography standards.
